a:T2426a,<div class="specification">
<p>The electron micrograph shows part of a cell including a mitochondrion.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: Used with permission of McGraw-Hill Education, from <em>Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine</em>,<br/>J L Jameson <em>et al</em>., 16th edition, 2004; permission conveyed through Copyright Clearance Center, Inc.]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline how the structures labelled X and Y are adapted to carry out the function of the mitochondrion.</p>
<p>X:</p>
<p> </p>
<p>Y:</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain how ATP is generated in mitochondria by chemiosmosis.</p>
<div class="marks">[4]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>b:T446,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>X: large/increased SA area for ATP production/electron transport/oxidative phosphorylation/proton pumping<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>X: small/narrow intermembrane space for generating proton gradient (rapidly/steeply);</p>
<p>Y: contains enzymes for Krebs cycle/link reaction;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. protons pumped across inner membrane of mitochondria/into intermembrane space;</p>
<p>b. using energy released by flow of electrons/by electron transport/by electron carriers;</p>
<p>c. proton gradient established/maintained / proton motive force generated;</p>
<p>d. protons pass/diffuse back through inner membrane/membrane of cristae/to matrix;</p>
<p>e. through <span style="text-decoration: underline;">ATP synthase</span>;</p>
<p>f. ATP production coupled to flow of protons / ATP from ADP and Pi using energy from protons;</p>
<p><em>Marks can be awarded in an annotated diagram</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/1/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","1"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"20N.2.HL.TZ0.3"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"20N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","8-2-cell-respiration, 1-2-ultrastructure-of-cells"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>The mean mark for this question was only 0.6 – less than half marks. Many students did not recognise they had to describe adaptations so, for example, the large surface area of the cristae or the small volume of the intermembrane space had to be included together with the function carried out. Weaker candidates had forgotten basic ideas of what happens in the mitochondrion.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>This was answered more successfully by most candidates and there were some impressively detailed and accurate answers.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
